segments.role: Engineer or operator evaluating a slide-generation workflow
current_knowledge: Understands that agents can create slides
Has seen slide generators overclaim or produce clutter
stakes: Needs a deck pipeline that can be reviewed
Needs evidence to survive visual edits
success_criteria: Rendered slides can be visually inspected

likely_questions.question: How do we know the slides did not invent facts?
Why start with HTML instead of PPTX?

objections.objection: This may be too much process for a quick deck.

audience_pov_critique.what_they_care_about: They want to know whether the generated deck can be trusted and repaired without starting over.
audience_pov_critique.what_will_feel_unproven: They will doubt the process until validation and browser QA are shown.
audience_pov_critique.where_deck_sounds_like_us_not_them: Phrases about internal artifacts can sound tool-centric unless tied to repair cost and review clarity.
audience_pov_critique.required_reframe: Describe artifacts as ways to reduce review risk, not as paperwork.
